summary: AI assistance was used to architect and implement a C++ matching engine with flat price ladder, SPSC queue, and OS-level tuning considerations.

initial_user_goals: Achieve best precision on API down to nanosecond per order
Change: C++ is appropriate for HFT-style engine
Model real-world exchange constraints beyond REST latency and achive O(1) on every operation
Apply data structures (queues, hash maps, flat arrays)
Estimate system throughput and latency realistically

architecture_decisions.rationale: High-performance systems programming with minimal abstraction overhead
Binary price range enables direct indexing and eliminates tree structures
Maintains strict price-time priority at each level
Removes mutex contention between REST layer and matching engine

notes: REST layer is explicitly a demo interface; core matching engine is the performance-critical component.

summary: AI assistance was used to review and improve a C++ toy binary outcome matching-engine REST API. The work focused on preserving the original assignment scope while improving correctness, edge-case coverage, tests, emulator logging, documentation, and repo hygiene.

prior_ai_context.source: User-provided pasted transcript from a previous AI session
prior_ai_context.summary: The earlier AI discussion proposed a C++ fixed-ladder matching engine, described REST vs core latency separation, and later refined correctness, tests, and safety framing.

human_direction: Add better tests and emulator output
ai_assisted_changes: Order states introduced (OPEN, PARTIAL, FILLED, CANCELLED)
notes: Project remains a toy in-memory REST matching engine; no external persistence, accounts, or exchange connectivity.
